title: Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI) Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Sleep Quality Calculator — Simplified Pittsburgh Index
description: Evaluate your sleep quality with a questionnaire based on the Pittsburgh PSQI. Quick score on latency, duration, and efficiency.
intro: The **Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI)** is the most-used questionnaire in clinical research to assess sleep quality. This simplified version evaluates **key components**: latency (how long you take to fall asleep), duration, efficiency, disturbances, and subjective quality. A global score >5 indicates poor sleep quality.
keyTakeaway: **PSQI ≤5**: good sleep quality. **PSQI >5**: poor sleep quality. Components: latency (min to fall asleep), efficiency (% of time in bed sleeping), duration, and disturbances.
useCases: You sleep poorly and want an objective assessment of your sleep quality.
Your doctor asked for a sleep questionnaire as screening.
You want to measure if habit changes improved your sleep.
You suspect sleep quality affects your performance.
You track your sleep quality monthly.

fields.label: How do you rate your sleep overall?
Do you have daytime sleepiness or low energy?

faq.a: **5 or less** indicates good sleep quality. **More than 5** indicates poor quality. Maximum score is 21 (worst possible quality).

faq.q: How many minutes is normal to fall asleep?
faq.a: **10-20 minutes** is normal. Less than 5 can indicate extreme sleep deprivation. More than 30 minutes is considered **sleep-onset insomnia**.
It's the percentage of time in bed you actually sleep. **>85%** is good efficiency. If you spend 8 hours in bed but only sleep 5, your efficiency is 62.5% (poor).

faq.q: Is waking up at night normal?
faq.a: 1-2 brief awakenings are normal. If you wake **3+ times** or take over 20 min to fall back asleep, it may indicate a sleep disorder.
If your PSQI is **>10**, if you have insomnia 3+ nights per week for over 3 months, or if you snore loudly with daytime sleepiness (possible apnea).

faq.q: Does this test detect sleep apnea?
faq.a: **Not directly.** PSQI measures general quality. If you have loud snoring, breathing pauses, and excessive daytime sleepiness, you need a sleep study (polysomnography).

faq.q: How to improve my PSQI score?
faq.a: **Fixed schedule** (sleep and wake at same time), **no screens 1h before**, **cool and dark bedroom**, **no caffeine after 2pm**, **regular exercise** (not at night).

howToSteps.name: Answer for your last month
howToSteps.text: Think about how you slept in the last 4 weeks.

howToSteps.name: Enter objective data
howToSteps.text: Minutes to fall asleep, actual sleep hours, and hours in bed.

howToSteps.name: Evaluate quality
howToSteps.text: Answer about disturbances, subjective quality, and daytime sleepiness.

howToSteps.name: Review the result
howToSteps.text: A PSQI >5 indicates poor sleep quality. Follow the recommendations.

explanation:
## What Is the PSQI

The **Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index** was developed by **Buysse et al. (1989)** at the University of Pittsburgh and is the world's most-cited questionnaire for assessing subjective sleep quality during the last month. It evaluates **7 components**: subjective quality, latency, duration, efficiency, disturbances, medication use, and daytime dysfunction. Each component scores 0 to 3; global score goes from **0 to 21**.

## Score Interpretation

| Score | Category | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| 0 - 4 | Good quality | Restorative sleep |
| 5 | Cutoff | Suggest follow-up |
| 6 - 10 | Poor quality | Needs habit changes |
| 11 - 15 | Very poor | Doctor consultation recommended |
| 16 - 21 | Severe | Possible disorder, refer to specialist |

### Key Components

- **Sleep efficiency**: hours slept / hours in bed × 100. **>85%** is good.
- **Normal latency**: 10–20 minutes. Less indicates extreme deprivation; more than 30 indicates sleep-onset insomnia.
- **Adult duration**: 7–9 hours per NSF (National Sleep Foundation).

## When to Use / Common Mistakes

- PSQI **does not diagnose apnea**. If you snore loudly, have breathing pauses or severe daytime sleepiness, request polysomnography.
- **Self-reporting**: an Oura Ring or Whoop nicely complements objective data.
- **Sleep hygiene** (darkness, 18 °C / 65 °F, no screens 1 h before) drops score by 2–4 points.
- **Caffeine and alcohol**: affect sleep architecture even if you don't wake. Alcohol reduces REM.

example.title: Example: person with 6 h sleep and 30 min latency
steps: **Data**: latency 30 min (2 pts), duration 6 h (1 pt), hours in bed 8 h → efficiency 75% (2 pts).
example.result: PSQI = 10 indicates probable chronic insomnia. Recommendations: fixed schedule, no screens after 10pm, bedroom at 18°C (65°F), no caffeine after 2pm, exercise before 7pm. If it persists > 3 months, consult.

sources.name: Buysse DJ et al. (1989) - The Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index
